title: Rubus chlorocaulon
taxonomy: Rubus chlorocaulon is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It belongs to the genus Rubus, which includes blackberries and raspberries. The plant is native to China and is classified as a thorny shrub. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Rubus chlorocaulon is related to other fruit-bearing plants such as apples and pears. More specific classification information may be found in botanical databases and scientific literature.

intro: Rubus chlorocaulis, also known as the Chinese strawberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throat, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Rubus chlorocaulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Rubus chlorocaulis is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine as a component of various formulations, such as decoctions and poultices.
